## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so I've already walked you through the three pieces of PLC, but I wanted to give you a little go a little deeper, a little bonus for you and give you some what I'm calling skeleton scripts. So this is just sort of outlines to think about as you're scripting out your videos. And I do recommend you script them out, even if you don't deliver them from a script, even if you just create the script and write it down and get it all nailed and then sort of, you know, and then go from an outline or go from memory or just don't go from anything but just script them out to know what you want to say. Don't think you can just turn on the camera and just nail this because very, like, pretty much no one can do that. The way to do this right is to actually script them out. I write out word for word. I write out my entire script these days. And then I've how I deliver them over the years has been all over the place. I've sat there and had them a script printed out at my feet. I would look down and I just memorized two or three sentences and I look up and deliver them into the camera and then look down. That's awkward. But I've done it. I've done it on big white boards where I've written out bullet points on white boards. I've done it with huge post-it notes on the wall behind the camera. Nowadays for my pre-launch scripts and for my sales script, I actually use a teleprompter and I go word for word from the teleprompter. So there's a lot of different ways you can do this. But you absolutely, whether you deliver it word for word from the script, whether or not you still want to script them out. So you've thought long and hard. And again, I know I'm not supposed to be talking about working hard, but by now you realize that if you're going to completely change your life with a launch or change your business with a launch, there is some work involved. And so scripting these out, even if you just script them out to bullet point. But I actually like to go word for word. Then you want to share your, your launch story. And I like to think of this as they're sort of a, a short version of the story and PLC one. And then you tend to reflect back on that story or expand on that story as you go through PLC two and PLC three. ## [03:02 - 06:04]

pieces in each of the video that, that tie in together. But then you also have like the first piece feels like a standalone piece. And then then all of a sudden the second video is the second piece that feels like a standalone piece, but builds on that first piece. And the difference here between PLC two and PLC one is you're recapping the last video. That's the second bullet point. And tell what's what you covered in that in that first video. You want to do this quickly. You don't want to be boring. You want it to work for both the people that haven't seen PLC one, but also the people that watched it. So now you're just sort of reminding them what you covered. You want to recap the opportunity. Again, you can go look at PLC two. I went into the deeper quite a bit deeper on this. ## [06:05 - 06:55]

to not only own your product, but own the transformation that they're going to have, the transformation in a life. Then you want to pivot to the offer and create a soft landing.
